author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Added `Status::Abandoned` (wire spelling `abandoned`) to the
status enum and wired `jjf abandon <id>` as a thin
`run_set_status(Abandoned)` convenience analogous to `jjf
close`. Storage-layer changes:

- `record.rs`: `Status::Abandoned` variant + doc.
- `trailer.rs::parse_status`: accept `abandoned`.
- `lib.rs::compute_blocked_set`: abandoned deps behave like
  closed (don't block dependents).
- `lib.rs::list_ready`: exclude `Abandoned` UNCONDITIONALLY
  (no `include_abandoned` flag on `ReadyFilter`).
- `lib.rs`: `claim` / `block` / `unblock` / `unclaim` reject
  abandoned targets with `Error::Invalid`, same shape as
  closed rejections.
- Cache's `slug_index` already covers every status, so slug
  uniqueness against abandoned holders is automatic
  (`find_slug_collision`).

CLI: `Commands::Abandon`, `StatusFilter::Abandoned`,
`StatusArg::Abandoned`. `jjf update --status abandoned` works
(documented revival path: `--status open`). No
`jjf unabandon` inverse verb.

Docs: storage-format.md bumped v2.6 → v2.7 with full
changelog; cli-json.md gained a v2.7 → v2.8 section
documenting the new verb, status value, ready-set exclusion
contract, and error shapes.

Tests added: 6 storage integration tests (wire serde,
round-trip via `set_status`, unconditional ready exclusion,
slug-collision against abandoned holder, two-call
non-idempotency, abandoned dep doesn't block) + 14 CLI
integration tests in a new `tests/abandon.rs` (happy path,
JSON envelope, default-ls hides, --status all/abandoned,
ready exclusion under flags, show works, bad-id and
preflight matrices, slug-stays-claimed, revive via
`update --status open`, help docs).

Manual repro from `/private/tmp/.../qa/abandon-verify/repo`
matches every spec point in the ticket body: abandon → ls
hides → --status all shows → ready hides →
`new --slug junk` fails with `slug_collision` referencing
the abandoned holder.

# Recommendation

Land as-is. Closing the ticket.

# Confidence

High. `cargo nextest run --workspace` green; manual repro
matches spec; doc bumps follow the v2.5 / v2.6 changelog
pattern.

# Open follow-ups

None. The ticket's "optional `jjf unabandon`" was
explicitly skipped per orchestrator design call (revive
via `jjf update <id> --status open` instead).
